mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and lower discount rates, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is already high, markets infer tighter real financing conditions and a higher risk of policy restraint, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes do not show negative average 20-day forward returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE that exceeds recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which increases near-term equity drawdown risk and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if unemployment-breakout episodes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand acceleration through retail spending and housing turnover, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST are both above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with both series above trend do not generate positive 60-day forward returns on average, or if the hit rate falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO relative to prior months signals stronger industrial output and better corporate revenue momentum, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if rising INDPRO fails to correspond to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign of the relationship flips persistently across two consecutive 2-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weakening aggregate demand and lower expected earnings growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1.0%
failure_condition: Rejected if low-growth GDPC1 quarters are followed by positive 60-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not lower than baseline by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS gains alongside a falling UNRATE indicate durable payroll expansion and improving income flows, which support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if payroll gains paired with stable or lower unemployment do not improve 20-day forward returns versus baseline, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive monetary regime, a high FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL keeps real rates elevated and tightens equity valuation multiples, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if high-rate/high-inflation regimes fail to produce negative 5-day returns, or if the relationship is indistinguishable from zero after controlling for recent market volatility.
